title: DevTools Recon Exercise
when_to_use: When passively analysing any web application

steps.title: Open DevTools
steps.instruction: Open any website in Chrome or Firefox
steps.tip: Start with a site you know well — you'll recognise anomalies faster

steps.title: Launch Developer Tools
steps.instruction: Press F12 to open Developer Tools
steps.tip: Also accessible via right-click → Inspect or Ctrl+Shift+I

steps.title: Network Tab
steps.instruction: Go to Network tab and refresh the page
steps.tip: Check 'Preserve log' to keep requests across page navigations

steps.title: Inspect Headers
steps.instruction: Click any request to view its headers (Request and Response)
steps.tip: Look for Server, X-Powered-By, and missing security headers

steps.title: Check Tokens
steps.instruction: Look for cookies, tokens, and parameters in headers
steps.tip: Authorization headers, session cookies, and API keys often reveal authentication patterns

steps.title: Check Responses
steps.instruction: Check the Response tab for API data or hidden fields
steps.tip: API responses often contain more data than the UI displays. Look for user IDs, roles, and internal data

steps.title: Document Findings
steps.instruction: Note any API endpoints, server versions, or frameworks exposed
steps.tip: Build a site map from your observations. This feeds directly into your recon report
